seoTitle: How to Use Canva Presenter View for Zoom
metaDescription: Learn how to use Canva's Presenter View during Zoom meetings for smoother, more professional virtual presentations.
intro: Presenting a Canva design smoothly on a Zoom call takes more than just screen sharing. This tutorial covers accessing Canva's Presenter View and using it alongside Zoom to deliver a more polished virtual presentation.

steps.heading: Access Presenter View in Canva
steps.body: Open your Canva presentation and switch into Presenter View mode before your meeting starts.

steps.heading: Integrate Canva with Zoom
steps.body: Share the Presenter View window through Zoom's screen share so your audience sees a clean, presentation-style view.

steps.heading: Engage your audience effectively
steps.body: Use Presenter View's tools, like speaker notes, to stay on track and keep the presentation flowing naturally.

steps.heading: Apply best practices for both beginners and pros
steps.body: Practice switching between Presenter View and other windows beforehand so the transition feels seamless during a live call.

faq.question: Do I need a Canva Pro account to use Presenter View?
faq.answer: Presenter View is generally available on standard Canva accounts, though some presentation features can vary by plan.

faq.question: Can I see speaker notes while presenting through Zoom?
faq.answer: Yes, Presenter View is designed to let you view notes on your screen while your audience only sees the slides.

faq.question: Will my audience see anything other than the slides?
faq.answer: If set up correctly, sharing just the Presenter View window keeps notes and controls hidden from your audience.

faq.question: Does this work with other video conferencing tools besides Zoom?
faq.answer: Yes, the same screen-sharing approach generally works with other video call platforms, not just Zoom.
